You
You are a support engineer assisting a developer who has made a "messy" commit containing multiple logical changes and has some uncommitted work in progress. Your goal is to clean up the history by splitting the messy commit and incorporating the uncommitted changes into the appropriate place. The repository is located at `/home/user/workspace/messy_project`. The current history contains: 1. A commit with the description `wip: all the things`. This commit accidentally bundles three distinct changes: * A new feature in `src/logger.py`. * A bug fix in `src/main.py`. * An update to `README.md`. 2. Uncommitted changes in the working copy (revision `@`) which add a missing import to `src/logger.py`. Your objective is to rewrite the history to produce three clean, atomic commits with the following requirements: 1. **Commit 1**: Description `feat: add logger`. This must contain the creation of `src/logger.py` *and* the missing import from the working copy. 2. **Commit 2**: Description `fix: crash in main`. This must contain the fix in `src/main.py`. 3. **Commit 3**: Description `docs: update readme`. This must contain the update to `README.md`. The commits should form a linear history on top of the initial commit. The order of these three commits does not matter, provided they are atomic. The working copy should be clean (empty) after the operation. Use `jj` commands (`split`, `squash`, `describe`, etc.) to achieve this. Do not use `git` commands.

Everything printed\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# to the output or error channels will be visible to the user.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Note that there is a sibling hook called `post-applypatch` (also without further context)\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# which is run after the commit was created. It is useful to use the commit hash for further\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# processing, like sending information to the involved parties.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Finally, the `applypatch-msg` hook is called at the very beginning of the `git am` operation\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# to provide access to the commit-message.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Retrieve the path to the pre-commit hook script using the \"git rev-parse\" command.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:precommit=\"$(git rev-parse --git-path hooks/pre-commit)\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Check if the pre-commit hook script exists and is executable.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# If it does, execute it passing the arguments from this script (if any) using the \"exec\" command.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:test -x \"$precommit\" && exec \"$precommit\" ${1+\"$@\"}\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Be sure to exit without error if `exec` isn't called.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample::\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# A sample hook to prevent commits with merge-markers\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:#####################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# This example hook rejects changes that are about to be committed with merge markers,\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# as that would be a clear indication of a failed merge. It is triggered by `git commit`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# and returning with non-zero exit status prevents the commit from being created.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# Check for merge markers in modified files\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:for file in $(git diff --cached --name-only); do\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:    if grep -q -E '^(<<<<<<<|=======|>>>>>>>|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|)$' \"$file\"; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:        echo \"Error: File '$file' contains merge markers. Please remove them before committing.\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:        exit 1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:    fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:done\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# Exit with success if there are no errors\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# A sample hook to check commits created by `git merge`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#######################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# This hook is invoked by `git merge` without further context right before creating a commit.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# It should be used to validate the current state that is supposed to be committed, or exit\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# with a non-zero status to prevent the commit.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# All output will be visible to the user.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# Check if the pre-commit hook exists and is executable. If it is, it executes the pre-commit hook script.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:test -x \"$GIT_DIR/hooks/pre-commit\" && exec \"$GIT_DIR/hooks/pre-commit\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# Be sure to exit without error if `exec` isn't called.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample::\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# Check for \"DELME\" in commit messages of about-to-be-pushed commits\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:####################################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# This hook script is triggered by `git push` right after a connection to the remote\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# was established and its initial response was received, and right before generating\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# and pushing a pack-file.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# The operation will be aborted when exiting with a non-zero status.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# The following arguments are provided:\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# $1 - The symbolic name of the remote to push to, like \"origin\" or the URL like \"https://github.com/GitoxideLabs/gitoxide\" if there is no such name.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# $2 - The URL of the remote to push to, like \"https://github.com/GitoxideLabs/gitoxide\".\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# The hook should then read from standard input in a line-by-line fashion and split the following space-separated fields:\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * local ref - the left side of a ref-spec, i.e. \"local\" of the \"local:refs/heads/remote\" ref-spec\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * local hash - the hash of the commit pointed to by `local ref`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * remote ref - the right side of a ref-spec, i.e. \"refs/heads/remote\" of the \"local:refs/heads/remote\" ref-spec\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * remote hash - the hash of the commit pointed to by `remote ref`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# In this example, we abort the push if any of the about-to-be-pushed commits have \"DELME\" in their commit message.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:remote=\"$1\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:url=\"$2\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# Check each commit being pushed\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:while read _local_ref local_hash _remote_ref _remote_hash; do\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  # Skip if the local hash is all zeroes (deletion)\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  zero_sha=$(printf \"%0${#local_hash}d\" 0)\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  if [ \"$local_hash\" = \"$zero_sha\" ]; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:    continue\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  # Get the commit message\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  commit_msg=$(git log --format=%s -n 1 \"$local_hash\")\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  # Check if the commit message contains \"DELME\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  if echo \"$commit_msg\" | grep -iq \"DELME\"; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:    echo \"Error: Found commit with 'DELME' in message. Push aborted to $remote ($url) aborted.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:    exit 1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:done\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# If no commit with \"DELME\" found, allow the push\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# A sample hook to validate the branches involved in a rebase operation\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#######################################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# This hook is invoked right before `git rebase` starts its work and\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# prevents anything else to happen by returning a non-zero exit code.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# The following arguments are provided:\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# $1 - the branch that contains the commit from which $2 was forked.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# $2 - the branch being rebased or no second argument at all if the rebase applies to `HEAD`.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# This example hook aborts the rebase operation if the branch being rebased is not up to date\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# with the latest changes from the upstream branch, or if there are any uncommitted changes.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:upstream_branch=$1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:if [ \"$#\" -eq 2 ]; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  branch_being_rebased=$2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:else\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  branch_being_rebased=$(git symbolic-ref --quiet --short HEAD) || exit 0 # ignore rebases on detached heads\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# Check if the branch being rebased is behind the upstream branch\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:if git log --oneline ${upstream_branch}..${branch_being_rebased} > /dev/null; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Warning: The branch being rebased (${branch_being_rebased}) is behind the upstream branch (${upstream_branch}).\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Please update your branch before rebasing.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  exit 1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# Check if there are any uncommitted changes\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:if ! git diff-index --quiet HEAD --; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Warning: There are uncommitted changes in your branch ${branch_being_rebased}.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Please commit or stash your changes before rebasing.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  exit 2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# All good, let the rebase proceed.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# A hook called by `git commit` to adjust the commit message right before the user sees it\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:##########################################################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# This script is called by `git commit` after commit message was initialized and right before\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# an editor is launched.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# It receives one to three arguments:\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# $1 - the path to the file containing the commit message. It can be edited to change the message.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# $2 - the kind of source of the message contained in $1. Possible values are\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"message\" - a message was provided via `-m` or `-F`\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"commit\" - `-c`, `-C` or `--amend` was given\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"squash\" - the `.git/SQUASH_MSG` file exists\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"merge\" - this is a merge or the `.git/MERGE` file exists\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"template\" - `-t` was provided or `commit.template` was set\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# $3 - If $2 is \"commit\" then this is the hash of the commit.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      It can also take other values, best understood by studying the source code at\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      https://github.com/git/git/blob/aa9166bcc0ba654fc21f198a30647ec087f733ed/builtin/commit.c#L745\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# The following example\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:COMMIT_MSG_FILE=$1\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Check if the commit message file is empty or already contains a message\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:if [ -s \"$COMMIT_MSG_FILE\" ]; then\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  # If the commit message is already provided, exit without making any changes.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  # This can happen if the user provided a message via `-m` or a template.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Retrieve the branch name from the current HEAD commit\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:BRANCH_NAME=$(git symbolic-ref --short HEAD)\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Generate a default commit message based on the branch name\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:DEFAULT_MSG=\"\"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:case \"$BRANCH_NAME\" in\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  \"feature/*\")\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    DEFAULT_MSG=\"feat: \"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    ;;\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  \"bugfix/*\")\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    DEFAULT_MSG=\"fix: \"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    ;;\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  *)\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    DEFAULT_MSG=\"chore: \"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    ;;\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:esac\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Set the commit message that will be presented to the user.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:echo \"$DEFAULT_MSG\" > \"$COMMIT_MSG_FILE\"\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# This file contains repository-wide exclude patterns that git will ignore.\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# They are local and will not be shared when pushing or pulling.\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# When using Rust the following would be typical exclude patterns.\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# Remove the '# ' prefix to let them take effect.\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# --help"},"output":{"output":"Jujutsu (An experimental VCS)\r\n\r\nTo get started, see the tutorial [`jj help -k tutorial`].\r\n\r\n[`jj help -k tutorial`]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/tutorial/\r\n\r\nUsage: jj [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>\r\n\r\nCommands:\r\n  abandon           Abandon a revision\r\n  absorb            Move changes from a revision into the stack of mutable revisions\r\n  bisect            Find a bad revision by bisection\r\n  bookmark          Manage bookmarks [default alias: b]\r\n  commit            Update the description and create a new change on top [default alias: ci]\r\n  config            Manage config options\r\n  describe          Update the change description or other metadata [default alias: desc]\r\n  diff              Compare file contents between two revisions\r\n  diffedit          Touch up the content changes in a revision with a diff editor\r\n  duplicate         Create new changes with the same content as existing ones\r\n  edit              Sets the specified revision as the working-copy revision\r\n  evolog            Show how a change has evolved over time [aliases: evolution-log]\r\n  file              File operations\r\n  fix               Update files with formatting fixes or other changes\r\n  gerrit            Interact with Gerrit Code Review\r\n  git               Commands for working with Git remotes and the underlying Git repo\r\n  help              Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)\r\n  interdiff         Show differences between the diffs of two revisions\r\n  log               Show revision history\r\n  metaedit          Modify the metadata of a revision without changing its content\r\n  new               Create a new, empty change and (by default) edit it in the working copy\r\n  next              Move the working-copy commit to the child revision\r\n  operation         Commands for working with the operation log [aliases: op]\r\n  parallelize       Parallelize revisions by making them siblings\r\n  prev              Change the working copy revision relative to the parent revision\r\n  rebase            Move revisions to different parent(s)\r\n  redo              Redo the most recently undone operation\r\n  resolve           Resolve conflicted files with an external merge tool\r\n  restore           Restore paths from another revision\r\n  revert            Apply the reverse of the given revision(s)\r\n  root              Show the current workspace root directory (shortcut for `jj workspace root`)\r\n  show              Show commit description and changes in a revision\r\n  sign              Cryptographically sign a revision\r\n  simplify-parents  Simplify parent edges for the specified revision(s)\r\n  sparse            Manage which paths from the working-copy commit are present in the working copy\r\n  split             Split a revision in two\r\n  squash            Move changes from a revision into another revision\r\n  stat
